macro_segments.title: Derivation of Shomer and Yad concepts from Biblical verses and the debate over their application to non-seeds

micro_segments.title: Rav Chaviva's resolution of how shomer applies to neveilah despite not fitting the zera zerua model
The problem: shomer of fruit and non-seeds don't fit the zera zerua paradigm of planting
Resolution through three biblical verses allowing abstraction and generalization of shomer to seeds, fruit, and animal products

macro_segments.title: Debate between Rav and Rabbi Yochanan on whether yad applies to both tumah and hechsher

micro_segments.title: Rav's position: yad applies to tumah but not to hechsher
Rabbi Yochanan's position: yad applies to both tumah and hechsher
Two possible bases for the debate: textual interpretation of pasuk or conceptual understanding of hechsher as beginning of tumah
Principle that neither tumah nor hechsher apply while produce is attached to the tree

macro_segments.title: Minimum size requirements for yad and shomer: debate between Rav and Rabbi Yochanan

micro_segments.title: Rav's position: yad requires kezayis minimum, shomer requires kepol minimum
Rabbi Yochanan's position: yad and shomer work even below these minimums
Case of two bones with half-kezayis each under a roof: yad works despite being less than kezayis
Dispute between Tanna Kamma and Rabbi Yaakov on combining two half-kezayitim through yad
Rav aligns with Rabbi Yaakov (no combining), Rabbi Yochanan aligns with Tanna Kamma (combining allowed)
Case of thigh bone with kezayis of meat: examining minimum size requirement by yad
Distinction: when object is whole (biryah), no minimum size needed; when fragmented, minimum required

macro_segments.title: Question of whether multiple shomerim (protective coverings) can combine for minimum size

micro_segments.title: Mishnaic principle from onion example: no shomer on top of shomer; outermost shell is discounted
Rabbi Oshaya's question: can two separate shomerim combine if one split piece of meat has skin separated in parts
Multiple shomerim on separate fruits can combine if each one protects its own food
Case of grain row: each kernel is unified through mutual support, functioning as single protective layer
Resolution of grain proof via Shimon ben Shatach's mystical wheat allowing kezayis in single row

macro_segments.title: Debate between Reish Lakish and Rabbi Yochanan on whether hair functions as yad

micro_segments.title: Case of two bones where one opinion says yad works for less than kezayis, other says it requires kezayis
Reish Lakish: bone functions as yad but hair does not
Rabbi Yochanan: even hair functions as yad
Proof from skin with meat and protruding hair or sinew: hair transmits tumah
Reish Lakish's objection: if hair is shomer, then shomer on shomer violates rule
Resolution: hair penetrates through skin, filling holes, so not shomer on top of shomer
Rav Acha's challenge: how can tefillin writing be valid if parchment has holes from hair follicles
Resolution: ink passing continuously over microscopic holes is considered complete writing
Alternative resolution: hair functions as yad only when in multiples (malui), not as single strand
Example of wheat stalk hairs: multiple hairs function as yad to transmit tumah but do not combine

topical_tags.term: Rav vs Rabbi Yochanan debate on yad/shomer minimums
Reish Lakish vs Rabbi Yochanan on hair as yad
biryah (whole object, as opposed to fragments)
combination/tziruf of multiple items or coverings
malui (hairs or attachments on grain stalk)
